items.title: Revisiting Hebb: The Mechanisms of Repetition Learning
items.author: Philipp Musfeld, Klaus Oberauer
items.content: <article>Perspectives on Psychological Science, Ahead of Print. In 1961, Donald Hebb established a classic paradigm for studying repetition learning: He asked participants to remember several memory sets for an immediate serial recall task and repeated one set multiple times throughout the experiment. items.title: Artificial Intelligence and the Psychology of Human Connection
items.author: Ryan L. Boyd, David M. Markowitz
items.content: <article>Perspectives on Psychological Science, Ahead of Print. As artificial intelligence (AI) becomes increasingly embedded in social life, understanding its interpersonal and psychological implications is urgent yet undertheorized. This article introduces the machine-integrated relational adaptation (MIRA) model, a ...</article>

items.title: How U.S. Funding Restrictions Endanger the Future of Psychology: Consequences for Research, Training, and Clinical Care
items.author: Kaitlyn A. Kaiser
items.content: <article>Perspectives on Psychological Science, Ahead of Print. In 2025, U.S. policy changes imposed sweeping limits on the scope of research eligible for federal support alongside sharp reductions to science-agency budgets, threatening the foundations of psychological science.
